column1: # Layout matches arduino-esp32's "min_spiffs" partition scheme so an OTA
# from Arduino-firmware → IDF-firmware writes to the same slot the running
# bootloader expects (otadata at 0xE000
# fielded ESP32-CAM units running the .ino can't OTA to the IDF build —
# the dashboard's BLE OTA only updates the app bin
# table. Each app slot is 1920K — comfortable headroom for camera + BLE.
# coredump partition from arduino-esp32's layout is dropped here. The
# `storage` partition below reclaims the 192 KB flash tail (ota_1 ends at
# 0x3D0000; the 4 MB part runs to 0x400000) as a LittleFS student drive —
# see main/fs_svc.c. No existing offset moves
# above holds: a fielded unit OTAs the app bin into the same ota_0/ota_1
# slots regardless of whether its flashed partition table carries `storage`.
# OTA never rewrites the partition table
# onto this build keeps its old table (no `storage`); fs_svc probes for the
# partition by label at boot and reports the fs unavailable when absent.
# Name
nvs
otadata
ota_0
ota_1
storage
